Suggested subject: Great connecting — SiteZeus resources & next steps

Hi Mike,

Thank you for hopping on the call today.

Mike and Melanie, really enjoyed the conversation today. The dual brand development committee situation David is chairing makes a lot of sense as a starting point — having franchisees able to log in, run a cannibalization report, and walk into a conversation with Dine with actual data behind them is exactly what our impact model is built for. The 93% accuracy on transfer estimates and the fact that our president has served as an expert witness in franchise disputes should give the co-op some real confidence there.

On the data question you raised — good news is the cannibalization piece doesn't require franchisees to share sales figures. The percentage-based transfer output works independently, so that's not a blocker to getting started. I'll put together pricing for both a cannibalization-focused starting point and a version that layers in the broader location intelligence, so you have both options in front of you.

Next Steps

  • Will to send pricing for two options: (1) cannibalization/impact-only starting point and (2) full Locate module with location intelligence add-on
  • Will to outline a go-live plan based on today's feedback

Relevant Resources

  • Sales Impact & Cannibalization — Directly supports the dual brand dispute use case Mike described — franchisees needing data to respond when Dine sends development letters.
  • Franchise Growth Webinar — Relevant given the co-op structure and franchisee adoption angle Mike and Melanie are trying to build out through CSCS.

Thanks,
Will Morgan

Hi Cwelch,

Below is a summary of the action items discussed during SZ | 60 minute call - Coleman Welch and Alan May.

Quick Call Review

  • Aligned on what the board needs — a concise, high-level one-pager explaining how SiteZeus drives sales forecasts and cannibalization estimates, not a deep technical dive.
  • Identified the model accuracy issues — missing stores and stale site attribute data — and agreed on a clean path to rebuild with just the high-signal variables (months open, store type).
  • Confirmed that franchisee name and agreement type have negligible signal in the current model, so those won't be prioritized in the rebuild.
  • Discussed protected radius variance (2–5 miles depending on franchise agreement) as context for how the model's default 5-mile ring was set.

Action Items

SiteZeus Team (Alan May)

  • Send Cwelch a concise one-pager (or short deck) summarizing how the SiteZeus AI model works and what drives the forecasts — suitable for board presentation. Deliver by end of day tomorrow.
  • Include a separate snapshot of current model accuracy and top attributes so Mike can decide whether to incorporate it.
  • Rebuild the model using only high-signal attributes (months open, store type), dropping low-signal variables. Will proceed once months open data is received.

Customer Team (Cwelch)

  • Pull and send months open data for all store locations (requires CRM export and reconciliation — flagged as the priority).

Key Decisions

  • Model rebuild will exclude low-signal attributes (franchisee name, agreement type, ingress/egress, review scores, etc.) and retain only months open and store type.
  • Only mature locations will be included in the model since the forecast requires a full year of sales data.
  • Board-facing materials should stay high-level — methodology overview and key data inputs only, not granular accuracy stats.

Next Milestone

  • Alan to deliver board one-pager and model accuracy snapshot by end of day tomorrow, ahead of the Thursday board meeting.

Best,
Alan May

Suggested subject: Great connecting — SiteZeus resources & next steps

Hi Juan,

Thank you for hopping on the call today.

Juan, Luis — really enjoyed the conversation today. The way you described growing each concept from the inside out — starting in South Florida and expanding outward as the brand builds recognition — is exactly the kind of phased approach where having the right data early makes a big difference. Whether it's finding the right outdoor/indoor setup for Dirty Rabbit, identifying dense Latino communities with the right income profile for Sala de Especho, or mapping fast-casual opportunities for Tacos Atarantados, each brand has its own criteria and we'd want to treat them that way.

Since your customer data lives separately across concepts and there's no unified loyalty program, we'll work with what you have per brand and build from there. I'll get a proper demo on the calendar so we can show you exactly how the scoring and white space analysis would look for your specific expansion markets. Looking forward to it.

Next Steps

  • Schedule a tailored demo — no date/time confirmed on the call, follow up to lock in a time

Relevant Resources

  • White Space Analysis Guide — Luis specifically mentioned wanting to identify the best-fit markets for each concept as they expand outward from South Florida.
  • Customer Insights — Market Intelligence — The team needs to understand Latino community density and income profiles to evaluate markets for Sala de Especho and Tacos Atarantados.
  • Restaurant Expansion Planning — With three concepts targeting different growth timelines — 1/year for Dirty Rabbit, 10-15 for Tacos, 10 for Sala de Especho — a structured expansion planning framework is directly relevant.

Thanks,
Will Morgan

Do not reply — generated automatically.

Hi Tyler,

Below is a summary of the action items discussed during Daily Sync - SiteZeus | Fresh Kitchen.

Quick Call Review

  • Walked through the scorecard together with Tyler Azzarelli (broker) for the first time — good alignment on how the scoring methodology works and what it's measuring.
  • Identified that the current customer profile segments cover ~42% of trade area population on average, and the 50% benchmark we set is intentionally aggressive — average stores are scoring around 84 as a result.
  • Miami stood out as an outlier: low customer profile score (16%), over-indexing on City Hopefuls (lower income), and structural site issues (parking, visibility) — all consistent with it being the lowest 2025 performer.
  • Agreed that adding Sunset Boomers and/or Suburban Boomers to the segment mix could get portfolio coverage closer to that 50% benchmark and better reflect the full customer base.

Action Items

SiteZeus Team (Aaron Bergland)

  • Evaluate adding Sunset Boomers and Suburban Boomers to the scorecard segments and show how that shifts scores across the portfolio.

Key Decisions

  • The 50% household threshold for a perfect segment score is intentionally aggressive — the team acknowledged this may need to be relaxed to better reflect actual average store performance.
  • Sunset Boomers and Suburban Boomers are being considered for inclusion in the scorecard to improve population coverage and scoring accuracy.

Next Milestone

  • Daily huddle cadence is ongoing — next session will review the updated scorecard with boomer segments added.

Best,
Aaron Bergland

Hi Matt,

Below is a summary of the action items discussed during 30 Minute Meeting - Aaron | SiteZeus - Matt Turner and Aaron Bergland.

Quick Call Review

  • Matt walked Aaron through his top 20 seed points spreadsheet and explained his prioritization logic — green sites (6) are highest priority, yellow (5) are secondary, red are deprioritized due to close PFLC proximity.
  • Agreed approach: run worst-case cannibalization scores only (not every nearby store) for each priority site, outputting the impacting store address + worst % impact into Matt's existing tracker.
  • Aaron committed to running these on Impact V2 / Zeus AI and delivering results before Matt's 4 PM CT meeting with Jeff.
  • Zeus AI training for Matt and Jeff is planned for the next couple of weeks — Aaron will proactively reach out when ready.

Action Items

Customer Team (Matt Turner)

  • Send Aaron the spreadsheet with green/yellow sites color-coded and any directional notes — today, ASAP.

SiteZeus Team (Aaron Bergland)

  • Run Impact V2 cannibalization studies for the 6 green priority sites (and yellow if time allows), returning worst-case impacting store address + cannibalization % — by 3 PM CT today.
  • Populate results into Matt's tracker spreadsheet on the right-hand columns (score per site on column L, worst % on column N).
  • Reach out to Matt and Jeff within ~2 weeks to schedule a formal Zeus AI platform training.

Key Decisions

  • Only the worst-case cannibalization score (single store, not combined) will be reported per seed point for today's deliverable.
  • Impact V2 on Zeus AI will be used going forward — Impact V1 is being retired.
  • Zeus AI training will be held off until platform refinements are complete, estimated ~2 weeks out.

Next Milestone

  • Aaron to deliver cannibalization results by 3 PM CT today ahead of Matt's 4 PM CT meeting with Jeff.

Best,
Aaron Bergland

Hi Tyler,

Below is a summary of the action items discussed during Daily Sync - SiteZeus | Fresh Kitchen.

Quick Call Review

  • Reviewed Fresh Kitchen's customer profile using 90K uploaded records — strong indexing on Upper Suburban Diverse Families (2x likelihood) and Blue Collar Suburbs, with Educated Urbanites flagged as likely underrepresented due to digital-only data skew.
  • Compared Fresh Kitchen's profile against Cava and Panera as proxies, confirming a broadly attractive and diverse customer base that supports expansion into major markets.
  • Agreed that the scorecard will weight customer profile data and demographic data at 50/50, reflecting Fresh Kitchen's balanced lunch/dinner split.
  • Identified an opportunity to run store-level customer profile uploads to enable more granular market analysis by location.

Action Items

SiteZeus Team (Aaron Bergland)

  • Schedule and conduct full platform training session with the broader Fresh Kitchen team after the current white space analysis is complete.
  • Re-run customer profile upload segmented by store (using the 'most frequent store' variable already in the data) to enable location-level analysis.

Key Decisions

  • Scorecard will be weighted 50% customer profile data and 50% demographic data to reflect the lunch/dinner balance across Fresh Kitchen locations.
  • Educated Urbanites will be kept in the customer profile despite low showing in uploaded data, as mobile data and market logic support their relevance for expansion markets.
  • Sunset Boomers will be excluded from the profile for now but flagged as a potential market share opportunity to revisit once the team is trained.

Next Milestone

  • Full team training session to be scheduled after white space analysis is delivered, with Friday 6/19 (Juneteenth) noted as an office closure affecting the current timeline.

Best,
Aaron Bergland

  • Explored adding Sunset Boomers and Suburban Boomers to the segment mix, which would push the portfolio closer to the 50% population threshold and better reflect stores like St. Pete, UTC, and Boca.

Action Items

SiteZeus Team (Aaron Bergland)

  • Model the scorecard with Suburban Boomers and Sunset Boomers added to the segment mix and show how scores shift across the portfolio.
  • Evaluate whether to lower the benchmark from 50% closer to the actual portfolio average (~42%) to make scoring less aggressive and more predictive.

Key Decisions

  • The current 50% population benchmark for customer profile scoring is too aggressive given the portfolio average sits around 42% — the team agreed this needs to be revisited.
  • Suburban Boomers and Sunset Boomers are worth adding to the segment model to improve population coverage, even if they aren't primary target segments.

Next Milestone

  • Daily huddle cadence continues — next session will review the updated scorecard with revised segments and benchmark adjustments.

Best,
Aaron Bergland
